This family is a large group of flowering plants that includes many economically important species such as potatoes, tomatoes, and peppers. Solanum unilobum is further classified as a species within the genus Solanum, which comprises around 1,500 to 2,000 species worldwide. The genus Solanum is characterized by its flowers, which typically have five petals, and its fruit, which is often a berry. Solanum unilobum is native to South America and is known for its unique characteristics and growth habits.

intro: Solanum unilobum is a species of flowering plant in the nightshade family, native to Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Solanum unilobum include solasodine, solanine, and glycoalkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Solanum unilobum is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture for the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, colds, and skin infections.
